notes: all combat numbers are simulator output on unverified constants
checkpoint levels are provisional (no level timeline in the match feed)
evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias

this build stacks over +100% attack speed; the sim now caps attacks/sec at the stated 3.0 (Cursed Ring tooltip), so sustained-DPS is no longer uncapped — the exact cap interaction still merits a practice-mode check

coachLine: Sim-optimal for sustained DPS: first spike lands around minute 12 (Sky Splitter), third item by minute 24. Take Demiurge: +23.9% on this kit’s main output at minute 15.

all.name: Marrow
all.note: Marrow (Major): missing-health-dependent omnivamp; no sustain-uptime model yet

all.name: Nihil
all.note: Nihil (Major): accrual rate depends on combat uptime (how long Doom persists per fight), which the closed-form sim cannot observe

all.name: Aion
all.note: Aion (Major): economy effect; valuable but outside combat objectives (revisit with gold curves)

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

augments.name: Press the Advantage
augments.note: reworks Rally Point into a team fire-rate aura; team-side and takedown-conditional

augments.name: Scorched Earth
augments.note: napalm zone DoTs; zone uptime and overlap unobservable

augments.name: Heavy Caliber
augments.note: basic-attack restructure (timer set to 0.75s, magazine 6); not mappable onto the attack-speed model

offMeta.honestAbsence: no defensible off-meta option this patch (no underexplored item clears the 8% objective edge vs the popular build)

metaBuilds.title: Lethality DPS Carry
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 24 — about 82 auto-attack DPS in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees 2.1× the sustained auto DPS: try Sky Splitter over Malady (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Lethality DPS Carry
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 24 — about 78 auto-attack DPS in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees 2.3× the sustained auto DPS: try Sky Splitter over Malady (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Lethality Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 24 — about 625 damage in one combo in the sim.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Sparrow ramps on auto-attacks — pressure their positioning before they itemize, and armor blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Deathstalker: their 3s all-in loses 36% of its bite; you give up 31% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Murdock deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Deathstalker: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 31% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Revenant ramps on auto-attacks — pressure their positioning before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Deathstalker: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 31% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Drongo deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Deathstalker: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 31% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Skylar ramps on auto-attacks — pressure their positioning before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Deathstalker: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 31% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. TwinBlast ramps on auto-attacks — pressure their positioning before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Deathstalker: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 31% of your damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “Press the Advantage” ⇒ on-hit; the sim can’t model this augment — steered by the declared playstyle + field evidence — field carry 48.1% over 1,700 games, magnitude not simulated.
